articles.title: CEC Entertainment Names Chris Monroe as EVP and Chief Financial Officer
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese parent company CEC Entertainment has appointed Chris Monroe as its new Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer. Leadership changes at one of the largest FEC chains in North America are closely watched across the industry as they signal strategic and financial direction. This hire may influence CEC's expansion plans, franchising strategy, and capital allocation in the coming years.

articles.title: Jay Gee's Fun Center Completes Arcade Upgrade with Betson Enterprises
articles.summary: Jay Gee's Ice Cream & Fun Center in Methuen, Massachusetts partnered with Betson Enterprises to overhaul its arcade game room, reflecting a broader trend of FEC operators investing in modernized game floors. Betson is one of the industry's leading distributors of amusement equipment, making this a useful case study for operators considering similar upgrades. The project demonstrates how even mid-size FECs can elevate their guest experience through strategic equipment partnerships.

articles.title: Wainwright Family Enterprises Promotes Matt Freed to COO Amid LBI Expansion
articles.summary: Wainwright Family Enterprises, operator of Fantasy Island Amusement Park and Thundering Surf Waterpark on Long Beach Island, has elevated Matt Freed to Chief Operating Officer as the company grows its attractions portfolio. The promotion signals a maturing organizational structure to support continued expansion of the family-owned FEC group. Operators of multi-venue family entertainment businesses will find this leadership model relevant as they scale.

articles.title: New Family Entertainment Center Coming to Mt. Juliet, Tennessee
articles.summary: A new family entertainment center is planned for Mt. Juliet, a fast-growing suburb east of Nashville, signaling continued investor confidence in the FEC sector in secondary markets. The development adds to a wave of new FEC openings across the Sun Belt as operators target suburban communities with rising disposable income and younger demographics. FEC operators and investors tracking new market entries will want to monitor this project's progress.

articles.title: Family Indoor Entertainment Centres Market Analysis Released
articles.summary: A new market analysis covering the family indoor entertainment centre sector has been published, offering data on growth trends, competitive landscape, and consumer demand drivers. Reports like this are essential reading for FEC operators and investors evaluating market positioning and capital deployment opportunities. The analysis likely covers key segments including arcades, bowling, trampoline parks, and laser tag.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Market Trends in India Show Strong Growth Signals
articles.summary: A new report highlights emerging trends in India's indoor entertainment market, reflecting rapid urbanization, a growing middle class, and increased appetite for out-of-home family experiences. For FEC suppliers and international operators, India represents one of the most significant untapped growth markets globally. This overview is useful context for those evaluating international expansion or equipment export opportunities.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Closes Coral Springs Location After 12-Year Run
articles.summary: Monster Mini Golf has shut down its Coral Springs, Florida location after more than a decade in operation, underscoring the ongoing challenges facing themed indoor mini golf concepts in competitive markets. The closure is a cautionary signal for FEC operators about lease economics, concept refresh cycles, and the need to evolve the guest experience. Operators in similar concepts should assess their own differentiation strategies in light of this closure.

articles.title: Monster Mini Golf Shutters Coral Springs After 12 Years
articles.summary: Monster Mini Golf's Coral Springs franchise has permanently closed, with the ownership citing the end of its run after 12 years in the market. The closure highlights the lifecycle pressures facing single-concept indoor entertainment venues, particularly themed mini golf, as consumer preferences evolve. FEC operators should take note of the importance of diversifying their entertainment mix to sustain long-term viability.

articles.title: Tenpin Opens New Entertainment Centre in Blackpool, UK
articles.summary: Tenpin, one of the UK's leading bowling and entertainment center chains, is now taking bookings for its new Blackpool venue ahead of a June opening. The new center will add bowling, gaming, and entertainment options to one of the UK's most popular leisure destinations. This opening is relevant for operators tracking the continued expansion of multi-attraction FEC formats in established tourist markets.

articles.title: New FEC 'Gameland' Opens in Cookstown with VR, Sim Racing, and Retro Gaming
articles.summary: A new family entertainment venue called Gameland has opened in Cookstown, Northern Ireland, offering a blend of virtual reality, sim racing simulators, and retro arcade gaming under one roof. The multi-format concept reflects a growing trend of FEC operators combining cutting-edge VR with nostalgia-driven retro gaming to broaden their audience appeal. This is a useful format case study for operators considering diversifying their attraction mix.

articles.title: Black Bunny FEC Blends Gaming, Adventure, and Family Entertainment in Dubai
articles.summary: Black Bunny, a new entertainment venue in Dubai, is combining gaming, adventure activities, and family-focused attractions in a single destination, positioning itself in one of the world's most competitive leisure markets. The venue's multi-concept approach mirrors what leading FEC operators globally are adopting to drive repeat visitation. Operators and investors in the Middle East market or those tracking international FEC design trends will find this relevant.

articles.title: 23 Jump Street Trampoline Park Opens at Cape Town's Rivergate Retail Center
articles.summary: 23 Jump Street has opened a new high-energy family entertainment venue at Rivergate in Cape Town, South Africa, bringing trampoline-based attractions to a major retail and leisure destination. The opening reflects the continued global expansion of trampoline park concepts into retail-adjacent locations, a proven model for driving foot traffic and secondary spend. FEC operators evaluating anchor-tenant retail strategies will find this opening instructive.

articles.title: Former Grocery Store to Be Converted into Barcade and Market in Coldwater, Michigan
articles.summary: A former Family Fare grocery building in Coldwater, Michigan has been approved for conversion into a barcade and market concept, illustrating the growing trend of repurposing large retail spaces for entertainment use. This adaptive reuse approach is increasingly common as FEC operators and entrepreneurs seek affordable, high-square-footage locations outside traditional mall settings. The project is a practical example of how vacant big-box retail can be creatively redeveloped for the FEC market.

articles.title: Claw Arcade Facility Heads to City Planning Panel for Approval
articles.summary: A claw machine-focused arcade facility is going before a city planning commission for approval, reflecting the rising popularity of claw and prize-redemption arcade formats as standalone business concepts. The claw machine segment has seen explosive growth, driven by social media virality and strong revenue-per-square-foot economics. FEC operators and investors tracking emerging arcade sub-categories should monitor this format's continued development.

articles.title: Kissel Entertainment Becomes First Carnival Certified Under iROC Safety Program
articles.summary: Kissel Entertainment has made history as the first carnival operator to receive International Ride Operator Certification from International Ride Training, the only third-party ride operator certification program in the world. This milestone raises the bar for operational safety standards across the amusement and entertainment industry. FEC operators running rides or attractions should be aware of this certification as it may become an industry benchmark for safety compliance.

articles.title: PortAventura and Vekoma Launch WeRiseUp Mixed Reality Studio
articles.summary: PortAventura World and ride manufacturer Vekoma have spun off a new independent company, WeRiseUp, specializing in integrating mixed reality experiences into existing physical attractions to extend their lifecycles and drive repeat visitation. The new studio targets theme parks and cultural heritage sites, offering a technology overlay that can refresh aging attractions without full capital replacement. FEC operators with legacy attractions should watch this development as the technology becomes more accessible.

articles.title: Six Flags Fiesta Texas Launches Daily Summer Operations with Extended Hours and Drone Show
articles.summary: Six Flags Fiesta Texas has kicked off daily summer operations with extended evening hours through August 9 and added a nightly drone spectacular as a new entertainment offering. The addition of drone shows as a low-infrastructure evening entertainment option is a trend FEC and park operators can evaluate as an alternative to fireworks. Extended operating hours and evening programming are proven strategies for increasing per-guest spend and annual attendance.

articles.title: Enchanted Parks Offers Free Admission to Military Members and Veterans This Memorial Day
articles.summary: Enchanted Parks is providing complimentary admission to active military, veterans, National Guard, and reserve members across its parks during Memorial Day weekend. Military appreciation promotions are a well-established FEC marketing tactic that drives goodwill, group attendance, and secondary revenue from accompanying family members. Operators looking for seasonal promotional frameworks ahead of the summer season can take note of this approach.

articles.title: SimEx-Iwerks Launches Next-Generation 4D Immersion Theater at Shedd Aquarium
articles.summary: SimEx-Iwerks has unveiled an advanced 4D theater at Chicago's Shedd Aquarium featuring expanded side-wall imagery, choreographed sensory effects, AR pre-show experiences, and updated seating technology. The installation demonstrates how immersive cinema technology is evolving beyond traditional 4D formats to incorporate AR and multi-sensory environmental storytelling. FEC operators considering immersive theater attractions or upgrades to existing 4D systems should evaluate this as a benchmark for next-generation installations.

articles.title: Parques Reunidos Outlines Digital Innovation Strategy for Guest Experience Enhancement
articles.summary: Parques Reunidos, one of Europe's largest leisure park operators, has detailed its approach to using digital tools to enhance the guest experience across its portfolio of parks and attractions. The strategy covers areas relevant to FEC operators including mobile integration, data-driven personalization, and operational efficiency. Understanding how a major multi-site operator is deploying digital solutions provides valuable benchmarks for FEC operators of all sizes.

articles.title: ThisPlays Launches AI-Powered Photobooth 'The Arch' in Partnership with Pathé Cinemas
articles.summary: ThisPlays International has introduced The Arch, a space-efficient AI-powered photobooth concept designed for high-traffic venues, debuting in an exclusive three-year nationwide rollout with European cinema chain Pathé. The system uses AI to continuously personalize the visitor experience and is designed specifically for venues where floor space is at a premium. FEC operators looking to add revenue-generating experiential photo moments without sacrificing significant floor area should evaluate this concept.

articles.title: Bubble Planet Immersive Exhibition Opens in Cologne with Ball Pit, VR, and Optical Illusions
articles.summary: Semmel Exhibitions has opened its Bubble Planet touring experience in Cologne, featuring twelve themed spaces including a giant ball pit, VR elements, an Infinity Room, and large-scale bubble installations designed for families and social-media-oriented visitors. The format's success across German cities highlights strong consumer demand for social, shareable, and family-oriented immersive experiences. FEC operators considering pop-up or permanent immersive experience formats should take note of this touring model's scalability.

articles.title: Hasbro and Little Lion Entertainment Share Insights on Bringing Game IP to Life in LBE
articles.summary: Experts from Hasbro and Little Lion Entertainment discussed how to successfully translate game intellectual property into location-based entertainment experiences, covering audience diversity, brand authenticity, and emerging LBE trends at the Blooloop Festival of Innovation. IP-based FEC attractions are a growing segment, and understanding how major licensors think about LBE partnerships can help operators negotiate and develop more compelling branded experiences. The discussion offers strategic framing for FEC operators exploring branded or licensed attraction concepts.

articles.title: Context-Led Destination Design Is Becoming the New Standard for Attractions Development
articles.summary: A new analysis argues that the most commercially successful attractions globally are those rooted in local cultural, environmental, and social context rather than exported copy-paste concepts or IP overlays. Three converging forces — cultural differentiation, shifting developer expectations, and guest demand for authentic experiences — are driving this change in approach to attraction design. FEC operators and developers planning new builds or major renovations should consider how local identity and authenticity can strengthen their concept's long-term appeal.

articles.title: Cinema Developer Bets on 'World's Largest' Screen in Clearwater, Florida
articles.summary: A cinema-focused developer is investing in a large-format screen attraction in Clearwater, Florida, positioning it as a major experiential entertainment destination in the competitive Tampa Bay market. Large-format and premium cinema experiences are increasingly overlapping with the FEC and location-based entertainment space, competing directly for the same leisure dollar. FEC operators in the region should be aware of this new entertainment draw when planning their own programming and competitive positioning.

articles.title: Peppa Pig Theme Park DFW Launches Summer Events Including New Character Meet-and-Greet
articles.summary: Peppa Pig Theme Park Dallas-Fort Worth is rolling out a summer programming slate featuring a new Miss Rabbit meet-and-greet and themed events including Bubblepalooza, targeting young families with limited-time experiences throughout the season. Seasonal programming and exclusive character experiences are proven strategies for driving repeat visits and extending the summer revenue window. FEC operators catering to the under-8 demographic can draw inspiration from this calendar-driven event strategy.

articles.title: Savannah Roller Rink Closure Sparks Community Effort to Save Local Skating
articles.summary: The closure of a roller skating rink in Savannah has galvanized local skaters and community members in a campaign to preserve the facility, highlighting the cultural and social importance of skating centers within their communities. Roller skating rinks, a classic FEC format, continue to face real estate and economic pressures in many markets. Operators in the skating segment should monitor this story as a reminder of the advocacy value of building strong community ties.

articles.title: Parques Reunidos Expands Aquasplash Antibes Water Park by 40 Percent with New Attractions
articles.summary: Parques Reunidos is significantly expanding its Aquasplash Antibes water park on the French Riviera, adding nearly 14,000 square metres and four new attractions including a raft ride, free-fall slide, and a wave pool for the 2025 season. The scale of investment underscores how major operators are doubling down on waterpark capacity to meet surging summer demand. FEC operators with outdoor water attractions or those considering adding aquatic elements can look to this expansion as a benchmark for ROI-driven capital investment.
